# Process-Reliabilism

**Node Type:** synthesis
**Node ID:** 9f3ee067-adcf-418c-a60a-83e9ac571963
**Depth:** 5
**Terminal:** No

**Parent:** [Is the Credit Theory circular when defining knowledge?](is-the-credit-theory-circular-when-defining-knowledge-antithesis-86f4dfaf-0126-418f-a09a-e8c043983bdf.md)

## Content

**Knowledge requires that the belief is generated by a process statistically likely to yield true beliefs**, **A belief counts as knowledge if the process that produces it can be tested for reliability**, **Beliefs formed through unreliable processes do not qualify as knowledge**, **The process can be evaluated independently of attributing knowledge, avoiding circularity**
